features.summary: P&G delivered Q2 results largely in-line with expectations despite volatility, with 3% organic sales growth driven by 2 points of volume and 1 point of mix. Nine of 10 product categories grew organic sales, with Family Care up double-digits and strong performance in North America (4% growth) and Europe (4% growth). Core EPS grew 2% to $1.88, with the quarter including $0.02 per share of costs from transportation system disruption. The company returned $4.9 billion to shareholders through dividends and share repurchases.
features.management_outlook: Management maintains full-year guidance ranges of 3-5% organic sales growth and 5-7% core EPS growth, though expects to trend toward the lower end due to FX headwinds and softer market conditions. They anticipate stronger second-half performance with continued innovation rollouts across major brands including Tide, Crest, and others. China showed improvement with only -3% decline versus -15% last quarter, and management expects continued recovery though remains cautious about volatility.
features.risks: Foreign exchange headwinds have increased to $300 million after-tax impact ($0.12 per share) for fiscal 2025, significantly higher than previous expectations. Commodity costs remain a $200 million headwind, and consumer environments remain challenging particularly in China and Middle East markets. Geopolitical tensions, supply chain disruptions, and currency volatility continue to present ongoing risks to performance.
features.key_drivers: Strong innovation pipeline across categories is driving market share gains and premium mix, with successful launches like Charmin Smooth Tear, Dawn Powerwash, and whole body deodorant products. Volume growth of 2 points and positive mix of 1 point demonstrate healthy underlying demand, while productivity improvements of 260 basis points help offset cost pressures. North America's consistent 4% growth and Europe's recovery momentum provide stable foundation for continued performance.
